items.name: Therma-Sense-IoT-Edge-Computing-Machine-Learning-Microclimate-Intelligence-Framework
items.description: An end-to-end IoT edge-computing and machine learning pipeline that ingests indoor microclimatic telemetry via ESP8266, cross-validates with the OpenWeatherMap API, and deploys Scikit-Learn predictive neural networks for localized urban health and thermal stress diagnostics.

items.description: A robust machine learning framework for predicting the axial load capacity of Concrete-Filled Steel Tubular (CFST) columns. The project utilizes XGBoost optimized with Optuna and provides interpretability via SHAP values to analyze feature importance compared to existing benchmarks.

It also includes visualizations, scoring metrics, and scikit-learn performance benchmarks.

items.name: Code-of-Optimization-Enhanced-Machine-Failure-Classification-Using-Critical-Sensor-Features-study

Three models, including Light Gradient Boosting (LGBC), Random Forest Classification (RFC), XGBoost Classification (XGBC), and a hybrid voting ensemble, were developed and subsequently optimized using the Tasmanian Devil Optimization (TDO) algorithm.
